page: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with x86_64 architecture, featuring 192 vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. It also features 11,339 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier performance in extended instructions and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth and large block uncached write bandwidth both ranking in the top 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier throughput.

The D192lds_v7 is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require high parallel processing power but do not demand high memory-to-CPU ratios. The 2.0 GB per core memory allocation represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-bound databases but highly cost-efficient for raw compute tasks. It excels in static web serving, high-throughput Redis operations, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing. The massive 11,339 GB local storage capacity makes it well-suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, and large-scale build systems.
description: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores. Operating at 4.2 GHz, the x86_64 architecture includes 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The server features 11,339 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional cryptographic and compression throughput. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which accelerate matrix mathematical operations. This configuration is highly efficient for parallel compute workloads, static web serving, and high-throughput Redis operations, though its low memory-to-core ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive databases.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 11,339 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-core-count, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring massive local storage and advanced hardware acceleration for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors at 4.2 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
11,339 GB of high-capacity local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and compression benchmarks
Optimized for high-throughput parallel compute, static web serving, and Redis
